keys.description: How often CheckChargebackStatusJob polls Stripe for chargebacks in non-terminal states. Default hourly. Stripe disputes typically resolve in 30-45 days; hourly polling balances timeliness vs API rate limits.
Paddle polling cadence. Default 4h.
Adyen polling cadence. Default hourly. Adyen's dispute state changes are webhook-driven — polling is a safety net.
Square polling cadence. Default 4h.
PayPal polling cadence. Default hourly.
CustomWebhook polling cadence. Null = do not poll (rely entirely on webhook callback).
Default evidence deadline per network in days from filed_at. Provider-communicated deadline overrides when available (some providers push tighter deadlines during heavy dispute volume).

Auto-collect evidence via CollectChargebackEvidenceJob immediately on chargeback creation. Default on — pre-populates the evidence bundle for admin review. Off means admin must manually enter every piece.
Days-before-deadline at which ChargebackEvidenceOverdueNotification fires. Default 7 / 3 / 1 escalating cadence.
Scan every submitted evidence body for PAN patterns (13-19 digit strings matching card BIN prefixes). Default on. PCI-DSS Req 3.4 — refuse evidence containing card numbers. Off is emergency-only.
Rolling window for chargeback rate computation. Default 60 days — matches Visa VDMP + Mastercard MDMP measurement windows.

Visa VDMP threshold. 0.009 = 0.9%. Rate above this fires ChargebackRateThresholdExceeded (P1). Enrollment in VDMP triggers additional fees + monitoring; sustained breaches lead to merchant-account termination.
Mastercard MDMP threshold. 0.015 = 1.5%.
Amex Enhanced Dispute threshold. 0.01 = 1.0%.
Discover Merchant Dispute Program threshold. 0.01 = 1.0%.

Multiplier on the threshold for early-warning alerts. 0.75 = fires early warning at 75% of the network threshold (e.g. Visa early warning at 0.675% of the 0.9% threshold). Actionable for ops before crossing the network's official threshold.
Cron for MonitorChargebackRateJob. Default nightly 04:00 UTC.
Rapid Dispute Resolution auto-refund threshold. Chargebacks below this amount (in cents) are RDR-eligible for auto-refund. Default 5000 = $50. Tenant may override per-tenant via settings.
Networks that support RDR. Visa-only currently — Mastercard's equivalent (Dispute Resolution 2.0) has different mechanics. Extend when Mastercard DR2 is supported.
Minimum days of customer purchase history before RDR is offered. Default 30 — new customers are higher fraud risk, less likely to be a genuine dispute worth auto-refunding.
Default network chargeback fee in cents per network. Provider-communicated fee overrides when available (some providers pass the exact fee in webhook payload). $15-25 range typical.
Default retention window in years for chargebacks + chargeback_evidence post-decision_at (or filed_at when never terminal). Enterprise `chargeback_extended_retention` uses 10y.
Extended retention window when Enterprise entitlement is on.
Cron for PurgeExpiredChargebackDataJob. Default weekly Sunday 05:00 UTC.
Auto-generate + email monthly chargeback rate report to admins. Default on — actionable for merchant-account risk awareness.
Cron for monthly rate report. Default 1st of each month 08:00 UTC.
Channels for ChargebackFiledNotification. P0 — SMS included by default for immediate awareness. Tenant may drop SMS via override.
Escalating channel cadence for ChargebackEvidenceOverdueNotification per days-remaining bucket. Push added at 3 days; SMS added at 1 day.
Channels for ChargebackRateThresholdExceededNotification. P1 — push included for admin awareness (merchant-account termination is a business-critical event).
